source_comments: asupersync-36m6p comment #3080 (2026-04-07 current-head raw-sample capture headline)
asupersync-36m6p.22 comment #3087 (2026-04-08 bounded successor-packet publication slice)

methodology.comparison_strategy: same target dir comparator corpus; current-head raw-sample capture from Criterion sample.json across baseline, auto, and rollback on the eight-scenario dual-policy family
methodology.value_contract: current-head raw-sample median/p95/p99 deltas across the same multi-scenario corpus
commands: rch exec -- env ASUPERSYNC_GF256_PROFILE_PACK=scalar-conservative-v1 ASUPERSYNC_GF256_DUAL_POLICY=never CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-e5-long-v4-baseline cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark --features simd-intrinsics -- RQ-E-GF256-DUAL --sample-size 40 --warm-up-time 0.2 --measurement-time 0.2

rch exec -- env ASUPERSYNC_GF256_PROFILE_PACK=auto ASUPERSYNC_GF256_DUAL_POLICY=auto CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-e5-long-v4-auto cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark --features simd-intrinsics -- RQ-E-GF256-DUAL --sample-size 40 --warm-up-time 0.2 --measurement-time 0.2

rch exec -- env ASUPERSYNC_GF256_PROFILE_PACK=auto ASUPERSYNC_GF256_DUAL_POLICY=auto ASUPERSYNC_GF256_DUAL_MUL_MIN_TOTAL=32768 ASUPERSYNC_GF256_DUAL_MUL_MAX_TOTAL=32768 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-e5-long-v4-rollback cargo bench --bench raptorq_benchmark --features simd-intrinsics -- RQ-E-GF256-DUAL --sample-size 40 --warm-up-time 0.2 --measurement-time 0.2

candidate_results.summary: Retains the canonical current x86 default contract while the broader raw-sample successor packet now shows improved combined p95/p99 but regressed combined median, so closure remains open.
Still rejected as the current-head raw-sample refresh does not justify promoting mul auto defaults into the canonical contract.

closure_assessment.summary: This successor packet keeps Track-E on the eight-scenario RQ-E-GF256-DUAL-001..008 corpus and upgrades the broader evidence to current-head raw-sample capture. Combined p95/p99 improve versus baseline, but combined median still regresses and mul remains mixed, so the packet is current mixed-signal evidence rather than closure-grade proof.
blocking_requirements: Demonstrate materially better broader-corpus canonical auto behavior on current-head raw-sample capture instead of the current mixed result (combined median regressed, mul still mixed).
Show closure-grade broader evidence that keeps the eight-scenario raw-sample packet favorable without relying on manual env overrides.

Keep highconf_v1 as the narrowed not-ready guardrail, v2 as the historical directional packet, and v3 as the historical broader interval-proxy negative guardrail until a future closure artifact version supersedes this mixed-signal v4 packet.

findings: Current-head combined raw-sample summary versus scalar conservative baseline is mixed: p50/median regresses by 1.25%, while p95 and p99 improve by 5.60% and 7.06%.
addmul improves overall on current-head raw-sample capture (median -2.17%, p95 -3.15%, p99 -2.74%), while mul remains mixed/regressed (median +1.26%, p95 +0.63%, 6 of 8 median scenarios slower).
Runtime-override rollback also improves combined median/p95/p99 (-0.38%/-8.97%/-4.66%), but that path remains manual-env-override-unbacked and is not closure-proof for the canonical contract.
limitations: This packet supersedes the broader directional refresh lineage while keeping highconf_v1 as the narrowed not-ready guardrail, v2 as the historical directional packet, and v3 as the historical broader interval-proxy negative guardrail.
The broader raw-sample packet is still mixed-signal evidence rather than closure-grade proof because canonical auto retains a regressed combined median and mul remains mixed.
Manual-env-override rollback evidence stays informative but non-canonical because it is runtime-override-unbacked.
Any future closure attempt must publish a new artifact/schema version instead of mutating the v2, v3, or v4 packets in place.
